topic: How long does it actually take your team to fully onboard one new employee?
format: State the uncomfortable truth about how most Oman businesses handle this problem — be specific and honest, not preachy
post:
6–10 hours. That's how long it takes most Oman businesses to onboard a single employee — and most of them have no idea.

Here's what it actually looks like on the ground:

HR types the offer letter manually. Sends it on WhatsApp. Spends three days chasing a signature. Finance sets up payroll in a separate sheet. Someone else submits the work permit. MOL filings go onto a to-do list that exists only in someone's head.

Two weeks later, the employee still isn't on WPS payroll. And nobody's quite sure who was supposed to handle the SPF registration.

This isn't a people problem. It's a systems problem.

When every step lives in a different tool — or no tool at all — onboarding one person quietly burns 6–10 hours of admin time across your team. Multiply that by every hire you make this year. That's a significant chunk of time spent on work that really shouldn't be this complicated.

SmartPRO Hub brings contracts, WPS payroll, SPF, Ministry of Labour filings, and work permit tracking into one place. One login. One source of truth.

Onboarding stops being a fire drill.

How long does it actually take your team to onboard one person right now? Drop a number in the comments.
